title: Tanker report - Week 17
summary:
VLCCs
It has been a slower week as charterers successfully managed to take the steam out of the market. A number of COA liftings were covered but market tonnage had little to offer for and as the pressure built, rates have dropped over 15 points for 270,000 tonnes going long east. China has been fixed in the very low WS 60s and there is talk of WS 57.5 having been agreed for South Korea discharge although this ship was said to have been wanting this direction for possible dry dock afterwards.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: It has been a slower week as charterers successfully managed to take the steam out of the market. A number of COA liftings were covered but market tonnage had little to offer for and as the pressure built, rates have dropped over 15 points for 270,000 tonnes going long east.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: An active week in West Africa saw rates for 130,000 tonnes to Europe rise around 10 WS points with the market peaking at WS 92.5 and WS 90 has been repeated a number of times . It has been a more volatile week in the Black Sea with rates for 135,000 tonnes moving up in to the mid WS 90s compared to the W 85 level of a week ago.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, an attractive longish voyage from Sidi Kerir to Portugal went at WS 87.5, otherwise rates for cross Med have generally held in the very low WS 100s , with Black Sea rates hovering around WS 102.5 level. In the Baltic, a build up of tonnage has seen rates for 100,000 tonnes ease around 10 points to WS 80 with a Mediterranean run fixed at WS 75.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: Although tonnage was tight, rates initially held at WS 105 region before a replacement cargo from Scandinavia to US Gulf reportedly went at WS 120. However with a fair bit of fixing and failing rates for 55,000 tonnes from ARA to US Gulf are now assessed at WS 110 while Skikda to US Gulf recently went on subjects at WS 105.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: In the Middle East Gulf, it has been an uneventful week with the market for 75,000 tonnes seemingly settled at WS 85, while in the 55,000 tonnes AG/Japan rates have recovered from WS 100 level to sit now at around WS 105 with potential for further modest increases. The 37,000 tonnes Cont/USAC market started the week at around WS 175 but with a falling backhaul market leading to more ballasters, rates lost 10 points to sit now at WS 165 region.
